item.title: Version 0.20.4
item.description:
What's New in 0.20.4

  • Show a persistent green checkmark on the worker connection button when cloud upload is verified.

item.title: Version 0.20.3
item.description:
What's New in 0.20.3

  • Improved the History view in Settings — tidier spacing, and item actions now appear when hovering anywhere on a row

item.title: Version 0.20.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.20.2

  • Added a GitHub profile link and cleaned up the links in the About section

item.title: Version 0.20.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.20.1

  • Fixed pixelate in the annotation editor — it now correctly pixelates the selected area instead of drawing an empty box

item.title: Version 0.20
item.description:
What's New in 0.20

  • Double-click to edit — Double-click a preview card to jump straight into the editor (annotator for images, video editor for recordings). First community contribution, thanks @jamesqquick! 🎉
  • Upload from the annotator — A new Upload action in the annotation editor saves your annotations, uploads the full-resolution image to your cloud, and copies the link to your clipboard — without closing the editor. Shows a live progress spinner and a "Link copied" confirmation.
  • Low-resolution editor preview — The annotator uses a lighter preview to keep memory usage down on large captures. It's now an explicit setting (Settings → Screenshots → Annotation Editor, on by default), the preview is sharper than before, and a small info badge in the editor explains it. Exports are always full resolution.
  • Redesigned annotation editor toolbar — Save As, Done, and Upload now live in a native macOS Liquid Glass toolbar pill with consistent iconography, replacing the old sidebar button bar.
  • Smoother stacked-preview behavior — cards dismiss horizontally and overflow is trimmed more gracefully.
  • Removed the legacy capture-hide flow for a snappier capture path.
  • The floating preview overlay no longer sits on top of the annotation editor, video editor, or Quick Look — it hides while those are open and reliably returns afterward (fixes cases where it blocked clicks/scrolling).

item.title: Version 0.19
item.description:
What's New in 0.19

  • Zoom images in annotator
  • Smart redaction and blur feature

item.title: Version 0.18
item.description:
What's New in 0.18

  • Removed unnecessary color picker
  • Updated installation instructions in README.md

item.title: Version 0.7
item.description:
What's New in 0.7

  • After-capture actions — Choose exactly what happens after a screenshot or recording (independently): show the preview overlay, copy, save, upload to Cloud & copy link, open the editor, or pin. Set them up in Settings → Screenshots and Settings → Video.
  • Pin to screen — Keep a screenshot floating on top for reference. Pin from the preview card, then drag it anywhere; hover for copy/save/close.
  • Copy text from image (OCR) — Right-click a screenshot in the preview or a pinned window to copy any text in it.
  • Self-timer — Add a 3, 5, or 10-second countdown before a capture (Settings → Screenshots).
  • Color picker — Pick a color anywhere on screen from the menu bar; the hex is copied to your clipboard.
  • Overlay preferences — Choose which corner the preview docks to, auto-close it after a delay, and toggle close-after-drag.
  • Capture options — Mute capture sounds, hide the menu bar icon, and optionally include a window's shadow when capturing a window.

item.title: Version 0.16
item.description:
What's New in 0.16

  • Smoother arrow curving — Bending arrows is now twice as responsive, and arrows snap back to a straight line when you drag the curve near the center.
  • Redesigned background picker — Backgrounds, gradients, solid colors, and wallpapers now share one clean, consistent grid with a clear selection highlight. Default padding and corner rounding have been toned down.

item.title: Version 0.15
item.description:
What's New in 0.15

  • Non-destructive annotations. Edits are no longer flattened permanently — screenshots keep their original pixels and a re-editable .screendrop sidecar, so you can reopen and tweak annotations any time after saving.
  • Smarter clipboard & saving. After editing, the latest version is automatically re-copied to the clipboard and the existing saved file is overwritten in place — no more stale copies or duplicate files.
  • Cloud setup is much smoother. A secure upload token is generated for you (with Copy / Reveal / Regenerate), and Verify Connection now provisions your worker's database automatically — no manual migration step after a one-click deploy.
  • Worker update notifications. The app detects when your deployed Cloud worker is behind and shows a non-blocking "update available" notice with a how-to link.
  • Cloud settings redesign. Cleaner, less cluttered layout with native input fields, an inline token reveal, and a collapsible setup guide.

item.title: Version 0.11
item.description:
What's New in 0.11

  • fix Tahoe deployment version to v26

item.title: Version 0.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.1

  • Capture fullscreen, window, or custom area screenshots with global hotkeys (Option+1/2/3)
  • Annotate screenshots with rectangles, ellipses, arrows, freehand drawing, text, numbered circles, and pixelate/blur tools
  • Floating preview panel with screenshot stack for quick access to recent captures
  • Auto-copy to clipboard, auto-save to disk, and optional JPEG compression
  • Runs quietly in the menu bar with no dock icon clutter
  • Your own cloud uploading with Cloudflare R2 + Workers (10 GB free on R2 free plan)
